metadata.format: BNS Sections mapped to Constitution Articles with Victim Rights & Remedies
metadata.note: This mapping provides constitutional backing, victim rights, and legal remedies for major BNS sections

bns_sections.title: Murder
bns_sections.description: Causing death with intent to cause death or bodily injury likely to cause death

punishment.details: Death penalty or life imprisonment + fine

constitution_articles.title: Right to Life and Personal Liberty
constitution_articles.relevance: Protects victim's fundamental right to life
constitution_articles.text: No person shall be deprived of his life or personal liberty except according to procedure established by law

constitution_articles.title: Equality Before Law
constitution_articles.relevance: Ensures equal justice for victim regardless of status
constitution_articles.text: The State shall not deny to any person equality before the law or the equal protection of the laws within the territory of India

victim_rights: Right to justice and compensation
Right to be heard in court (through victim impact statement)
Right to compensation from Victim Compensation Scheme
Right to free legal aid if victim is poor
Right to counseling and psychological support
Right to information about case status

remedies_and_paths.name: Victim Compensation
steps: Apply to State Victim Compensation Board
remedies_and_paths.amount_range: ₹5-10 lakhs or more depending on state

support_organizations.name: One-Stop Centre Scheme
support_organizations.services: Counseling, legal aid, shelter for victims

bns_sections.title: Grievous Hurt

constitution_articles.title: Right to Life and Personal Liberty
constitution_articles.relevance: Protects bodily integrity and health

constitution_articles.title: Freedom to Practice any Profession
constitution_articles.relevance: Victim's right to work is protected if injury affects livelihood

victim_rights: Right to medical treatment at government expense
Right to compensation for medical expenses and lost wages
Right to pain and suffering compensation
Right to disability compensation if permanently injured
Right to criminal proceedings against perpetrator

remedies_and_paths.name: Civil Suit for Tort
steps: File suit claiming damages for negligence or assault
Recover compensation for pain, suffering, medical costs

bns_sections.title: Theft
bns_sections.description: Dishonestly taking moveable property without consent

constitution_articles.title: Right to Property (Amended)
constitution_articles.relevance: Protects victim's property rights
constitution_articles.text: No person shall be deprived of his property save by authority of law

constitution_articles.title: Right to Profession/Business
constitution_articles.relevance: If theft affects victim's business/livelihood

victim_rights: Right to file FIR at any police station (Zero FIR)
Right to compensation for stolen property value
Right to police assistance in recovery of property
Right to information about investigation status
Right to claim restitution from convict

remedies_and_paths.name: Police FIR and Recovery
steps: File written complaint if police refuses

remedies_and_paths.name: Insurance Claim
steps: Submit insurance claim with FIR copy
Provide proof of property and damage

remedies_and_paths.name: Civil Recovery Suit
steps: File suit for recovery of value of property

bns_sections.title: Rape
bns_sections.description: Sexual intercourse without consent or with consent obtained by threats/fraud/coercion

constitution_articles.title: Right to Life and Personal Liberty
constitution_articles.relevance: Protects bodily autonomy and dignity of rape survivor
constitution_articles.text: Includes right to dignity, privacy, and bodily integrity

constitution_articles.title: Equality Before Law
constitution_articles.relevance: Ensures equal justice regardless of victim's gender, caste, religion

constitution_articles.title: Prohibition of Discrimination
constitution_articles.relevance: Protects against discrimination in legal proceedings

victim_rights: Right to privacy and anonymity in case
Right to be examined by female medical officer only
Right to file FIR at any police station (Zero FIR)
Right to free legal aid
Right to counseling and psychological support
Right to compensation from State
Right to witness protection if threatened
Right to be informed of court proceedings
Right to rehabilitation assistance
Right to claim loss of wages and medical expenses
Right to file case even after marriage to accused

remedies_and_paths.name: Criminal Prosecution (Primary Remedy)
steps: File FIR at any police station (regardless of jurisdiction)
Medical examination by female doctor (Section 54 BNSS)

remedies_and_paths.name: Compensation from State
steps: Apply to District Legal Services Authority
Submit FIR, medical reports, proof of damages

remedies_and_paths.name: Civil Suit for Damages
steps: File suit for tort of negligence or intentional wrong

remedies_and_paths.name: Rehabilitation and Support
steps: Register with One-Stop Centre for counseling
Avail shelters under UJJWALA scheme if needed

support_organizations.name: One-Stop Centre (OSC)
support_organizations.services: Integrated support: medical, legal, psychological, shelter

support_organizations.name: UJJWALA Scheme
support_organizations.services: Rehabilitation of survivors of trafficking and sexual violence

procedural_protections: Testimony can be recorded in camera (in private)
Victim need not identify herself in court
No question about victim's past sexual history without permission
Cannot be subjected to polygraph test without consent

special_provisions.zero_fir: Can file at any police station regardless of jurisdiction
special_provisions.medical_examination: Only by female doctor, free of charge

bns_sections.title: Assault on Woman with Intent to Outrage Modesty
bns_sections.description: Criminal force or assault on woman with intent to outrage or knowing it will outrage modesty

constitution_articles.title: Right to Life with Dignity
constitution_articles.relevance: Protects dignity and bodily autonomy of women

constitution_articles.title: State Power to Make Affirmative Provisions
constitution_articles.relevance: State can make special provisions for women's protection

victim_rights: Right to file FIR with police
Right to be examined by female doctor only
Right to free legal aid
Right to compensation
Right to privacy in proceedings
Right to witness protection

remedies_and_paths.name: Victim Compensation
steps: Get compensation for trauma and damages

bns_sections.title: Cruelty by Husband or Relatives
bns_sections.description: Subjecting woman to cruelty, including harassment for dowry or conduct likely to drive her to suicide

constitution_articles.title: Right to Life and Dignity
constitution_articles.relevance: Protects woman's right to dignity in marriage

constitution_articles.title: Prohibition of Discrimination
constitution_articles.relevance: Prohibits discrimination on basis of sex

victim_rights: Right to legal protection from cruelty
Right to maintenance from spouse
Right to custody of children
Right to protection from harassment
Right to police intervention
Right to legal separation or divorce

remedies_and_paths.name: Protection Order Under Domestic Violence Act 2005
steps: Get Residence Order (right to stay in marital home)

remedies_and_paths.name: Civil Remedies
steps: File for divorce under Hindu Marriage Act

bns_sections.title: Dowry Death
bns_sections.description: Death of woman by burns or bodily injury within 7 years of marriage under circumstances suggesting dowry harassment

constitution_articles.title: Right to Life
constitution_articles.relevance: Right to life of women protected especially in matrimonial context

constitution_articles.title: State Power to Make Special Provisions for Women
constitution_articles.relevance: State enacted special protection against dowry

victim_rights: Right to justice and punishment of perpetrators
Right to compensation to heirs from State
Right to compensation from dowry-demander
Right to insurance claims
Right to investigation without delay

remedies_and_paths.name: Criminal Prosecution (Mandatory)
remedies_and_paths.special_note: Strict liability - burden of proof on accused to prove innocence

remedies_and_paths.name: Dowry Prohibition Act Case
steps: File case under Dowry Prohibition Act

remedies_and_paths.name: Compensation to Heirs
steps: Get compensation for loss of bread-earner
Compensation for dependent children and parents

remedies_and_paths.name: Insurance and Financial Benefits
steps: Social security benefits for children and parents

special_provisions.custody_period: Police can custody for up to 14 days without magistrate's permission
special_provisions.fir_refusal: Police cannot refuse to file FIR - can approach superintendent
special_provisions.evidence_preservation: All medical records and evidence must be preserved

bns_sections.title: Extortion
bns_sections.description: Intentionally putting any person in fear of injury to induce dishonest delivery of property

constitution_articles.title: Protection of Property
constitution_articles.relevance: Protects property rights of victim

constitution_articles.title: Freedom of Profession/Business
constitution_articles.relevance: Protects business from extortion threats

constitution_articles.title: Right to Life and Security
constitution_articles.relevance: Protects victim from threats and fear

victim_rights: Right to file FIR against extortionists
Right to police protection
Right to get recovery of property/money
Right to compensation
Right to restraining orders against harasser
Right to witness protection if needed

remedies_and_paths.name: Police FIR and Criminal Case
steps: File written complaint if police refuses

remedies_and_paths.name: Protection Orders
steps: Apply to magistrate for protection order

remedies_and_paths.name: Recovery of Property/Money
steps: Court order for recovery from convicted person
remedies_and_paths.note: If convict cannot pay, victim can file execution petition

bns_sections.title: Assault
bns_sections.description: Making any gesture or preparation intending or knowing it likely to cause apprehension of criminal force

constitution_articles.title: Right to Life and Personal Liberty
constitution_articles.relevance: Protects from threat of force and intimidation

constitution_articles.title: Freedom of Movement
constitution_articles.relevance: Protects right to move freely without threat

victim_rights: Right to file complaint with police
Right to get protection order
Right to compensation for trauma
Right to restraining order

bns_sections.title: Cheating and Fraud
bns_sections.description: Deceiving any person and fraudulently inducing delivery of property or retention of property

constitution_articles.title: Protection of Property
constitution_articles.relevance: Protects property from fraudulent taking

constitution_articles.title: Right to Profession
constitution_articles.relevance: Protects from fraud in business transactions

victim_rights: Right to file FIR against fraudster
Right to get recovery of money/property
Right to compensation for loss
Right to restrain transfer of property
Right to civil suit for damages

remedies_and_paths.name: Civil Suit for Damages
steps: File suit for recovery of amount

support_organizations.name: Cyber Crime Cell
support_organizations.services: For online fraud and digital fraud

bns_sections.title: Criminal Breach of Trust
bns_sections.description: Being entrusted with property and dishonestly misappropriating or converting for own use

constitution_articles.title: Protection of Property
constitution_articles.relevance: Protects entrusted property of victim

constitution_articles.title: Right to Profession
constitution_articles.relevance: Protects business relationships from breach

victim_rights: Right to file FIR against breach of trust
Right to get recovery of property
Right to compensation for loss
Right to civil remedies
Right to preventive attachment of property

remedies_and_paths.name: Recovery of Entrusted Property
steps: Court orders recovery of specific property

fundamental_rights.title: Equality Before Law
fundamental_rights.application_to_victims: Ensures equal justice for all victims regardless of caste, religion, gender, or social status

fundamental_rights.title: Prohibition of Discrimination
fundamental_rights.application_to_victims: State cannot discriminate against victims based on sex, caste, religion, place of birth, or status

fundamental_rights.title: Right to Protection of Certain Rights Regarding Freedom
fundamental_rights.application_to_victims: Protects victim's right to freedom and liberty during legal proceedings

fundamental_rights.title: Right to Life and Personal Liberty
fundamental_rights.application_to_victims: Protects victim's dignity, privacy, bodily integrity, and mental wellbeing
landmark_cases: Kesavananda Bharati v State of Kerala - Right to life includes right to dignity
Selvi v State of Karnataka - Right to bodily autonomy

fundamental_rights.title: Right to Property
fundamental_rights.application_to_victims: Protects victim's property from theft, extortion, breach of trust, and fraud

directive_principles.title: Equal Pay for Equal Work
directive_principles.application_to_victims: If victim's livelihood affected, state must ensure equal treatment

directive_principles.title: Prevent Exploitation
directive_principles.application_to_victims: State must take steps to prevent exploitation and abuse of citizens

victim_compensation_framework.applicability: All states (though amount and procedures vary)
eligibility: Victim of crime committed after scheme notification
Victim must apply within prescribed time
how_to_apply: Attach FIR copy, medical reports, proof of loss

victim_support_services.name: NALSA Legal Aid
victim_support_services.services: Free legal aid, victim compensation assistance

victim_support_services.name: Child Helpline
victim_support_services.services: For child victims and abuse cases

victim_support_services.name: One-Stop Centre (OSC)
victim_support_services.services: Integrated support: medical, legal, psychological, shelter
victim_support_services.note: Present in major cities and districts

procedural_safeguards_for_victims.description: File FIR at any police station regardless of jurisdiction, transferred to appropriate station within 15 days
Victims below poverty line get free legal representation
Medical examination free of charge, conducted by officer of victim's choice (preferably same gender for sexual crimes)
Victim's identity protected in certain cases (especially sexual crimes)
Victim can present statement about impact of crime on them
If victim threatened, can request witness protection
While case pending, victim can apply for interim relief
Victim has right to know status of investigation and court proceedings

landmarks_cases.details: Delhi rape case leading to amendments in rape laws and victim protection measures

landmarks_cases.case_name: Budhadev Karmaskar v State of West Bengal (2006)
landmarks_cases.details: Case on right to life including right to food, shelter, education

landmarks_cases.case_name: Kesavananda Bharati v State of Kerala (1973)
landmarks_cases.details: Established right to dignity as part of right to life under Article 21
Right to bodily autonomy and refusal of invasive procedures

how_to_use_this_mapping.for_law_students: Understand each BNS section with constitutional backing and victim remedies
how_to_use_this_mapping.for_ngos: Use as framework for victim counseling and guidance
how_to_use_this_mapping.for_police: Reference for victim rights and procedural requirements
how_to_use_this_mapping.for_victims: Know your rights and available remedies
how_to_use_this_mapping.for_legal_aid: Use to provide comprehensive legal information
